# aperture-announce

> Running an L402-gated API behind Aperture but AI agents can't find it?
> aperture-announce reads your Aperture YAML config and publishes kind 31402
> Nostr events so agents can discover, pay for, and consume your service.

aperture-announce is a standalone Go binary for Aperture operators who want
their L402 services discoverable by AI agents. Point it at your existing
aperture.yaml, specify your relays, and your services appear in
`l402_search` results via 402-mcp. Unlike 402-announce (the TypeScript
equivalent), this tool requires no Node.js runtime — it's a single binary
that reads Aperture's native config format directly.

## Getting Started

```bash
go install github.com/TheCryptoDonkey/aperture-announce/cmd/aperture-announce@latest

aperture-announce \
  --config /path/to/aperture.yaml \
  --relays wss://relay.damus.io,wss://nos.lol \
  --public-urls https://api.example.com
```

Preview without publishing:

```bash
aperture-announce --config aperture.yaml --public-urls https://api.example.com --dry-run
```

## Key Concepts

- **Kind 31402 events** — replaceable Nostr events that advertise L402 services with pricing, capabilities, and payment method tags.
- **Aperture config parsing** — reads the `services` block from Aperture's YAML, extracting names, paths, prices, and capabilities.
- **Announce key** — a Nostr signing key, auto-generated and persisted to `~/.aperture-announce/announce.key` if not provided.
- **Relay publishing** — publishes to multiple relays concurrently; supports one-shot or interval-based re-publishing.
- **Auth levels** — capabilities can specify authentication requirements: `"none"` (freely accessible), `"freebie N"` (N free requests before payment), or omitted (payment required).
- **Token timeout** — capabilities can specify how long an L402 token remains valid via the `timeout` field (seconds).
- **Dynamic pricing** — services using dynamic pricing have their `price` tag omitted and carry `"pricing": "dynamic"` in the content capabilities; a `dynamic-pricing` topic tag is added to the event.

## CLI Reference

```
aperture-announce [flags]

Required:
  --config <path>        Path to Aperture YAML config
  --relays <urls>        Comma-separated Nostr relay URLs
  --public-urls <urls>   Comma-separated public URLs agents will use to reach your service (supports multiple transports: clearnet, .onion, Handshake)

Optional:
  --public-url <url>     Alias for --public-urls (single URL; backwards-compatible)
  --announce-key <hex>   Nostr signing key (auto-generated if omitted)
  --interval <duration>  Re-publish interval (e.g. 6h); default: one-shot
  --picture <url>        Service icon URL
  --topics <tags>        Comma-separated custom topic tags (appended to defaults)
  --dry-run              Print event JSON without publishing
  --verbose              Verbose logging
```

Environment variable fallbacks: `APERTURE_CONFIG`, `ANNOUNCE_RELAYS`, `PUBLIC_URLS`, `PUBLIC_URL` (single-URL alias), `ANNOUNCE_KEY`, `ANNOUNCE_TOPICS`.

## Kind 31402 Event Format

Kind 31402 is a **replaceable event** (NIP-33). The `d` tag serves as the unique identifier — publishing a new event with the same `d` tag replaces the previous one on relays.

### Tags

| Tag | Format | Description |
|-----|--------|-------------|
| `d` | `["d", "aperture-<hostname>"]` | Replaceable event identifier, derived from the public URL hostname |
| `name` | `["name", "<service-name>"]` | Short service name (e.g. `loop-rpc`) |
| `url` | `["url", "<public-url>"]` | The URL agents use to reach the service |
| `about` | `["about", "<description>"]` | Longer human-readable description of the service |
| `pmi` | `["pmi", "bitcoin-lightning-bolt11"]` | Payment method indicator. Always Lightning for Aperture |
| `t` | `["t", "<topic>"]` | Topic tags: `l402`, `api`, `aperture`, plus any custom topics |
| `price` | `["price", "<cap>", "<amount>", "<currency>"]` | Positional price tag per capability. Omitted for dynamically priced capabilities with no static fallback |
| `picture` | `["picture", "<url>"]` | Optional service icon URL |

The `endpoint` field in capabilities is a **cleaned base path** (e.g. `/v1/loop`) derived from the Aperture `pathregexp`, not the raw regexp string.

### Price Tags

Price tags use **positional elements**, not JSON:

```
["price", "quote", "500", "sats"]
["price", "swap", "500", "sats"]
```

Each capability from the Aperture config gets its own price tag. If a service has no explicit capabilities, the service name is used as the capability name.

### Content Field

The `content` field contains a JSON-encoded object listing capabilities with their endpoints. Each capability supports optional fields for auth, timeout, and dynamic pricing:

```json
{
  "capabilities": [
    {
      "name": "quote",
      "description": "quote via loop-rpc",
      "endpoint": "/v1/loop",
      "auth": "none",
      "timeout": 3600
    },
    {
      "name": "swap",
      "description": "swap via loop-rpc",
      "endpoint": "/v1/loop",
      "pricing": "dynamic"
    },
    {
      "name": "pool-rpc",
      "description": "Access pool-rpc",
      "endpoint": "/v1/pool",
      "auth": "freebie 5"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Auth Field

The optional `auth` field on a capability specifies its authentication requirement:

- `"none"` — no authentication required; the endpoint is freely accessible
- `"freebie N"` — the first N requests are free before payment is required (e.g. `"freebie 10"`)
- omitted — payment is required (the default L402 behaviour)

### Timeout Field

The optional `timeout` field specifies the L402 token validity period in **seconds**. When omitted, tokens do not carry an expiry.

### Dynamic Pricing

When a capability's price is determined at request time rather than statically:

- The capability carries `"pricing": "dynamic"` in the content JSON
- The corresponding `price` tag is omitted from the event (unless a static fallback price is also configured)
- A `["t", "dynamic-pricing"]` topic tag is added to the event to make dynamic-pricing services discoverable

### Mapping from Aperture Config

| Aperture YAML field | Event field |
|---------------------|-------------|
| `services[].name` | Used in `name`/`about` tags and as fallback capability name |
| `services[].pathregexp` | `endpoint` in content capabilities |
| `services[].price` | Amount in `price` tags |
| `services[].capabilities` | Comma-separated list → individual `price` tags and content capabilities |

## JSON Schema

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://github.com/TheCryptoDonkey/aperture-announce/schemas/kind-31402.schema.json",
  "title": "Kind 31402 — L402 Service Announcement",
  "description": "A replaceable Nostr event that advertises an L402-gated service with pricing, capabilities, and payment method metadata.",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["kind", "tags", "content"],
  "properties": {
    "kind": {
      "const": 31402,
      "description": "Nostr event kind for L402 service announcements."
    },
    "tags": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "NIP-01 tag array. Required tags: d, name, url, about, pmi, t, price.",
      "items": {
        "type": "array",
        "items": { "type": "string" }
      },
      "contains": {
        "description": "Must include a 'd' tag for replaceable event identity.",
        "type": "array",
        "prefixItems": [{ "const": "d" }, { "type": "string" }]
      }
    },
    "content": {
      "type": "string",
      "description": "JSON-encoded capabilities object: {\"capabilities\": [{\"name\", \"description\", \"endpoint\", \"pricing?\", \"auth?\", \"timeout?\"}]}."
    }
  },
  "definitions": {
    "d_tag": {
      "description": "Replaceable event identifier, typically 'aperture-<hostname>'.",
      "type": "array",
      "prefixItems": [{ "const": "d" }, { "type": "string" }]
    },
    "name_tag": {
      "description": "Human-readable service description.",
      "type": "array",
      "prefixItems": [{ "const": "name" }, { "type": "string" }]
    },
    "url_tag": {
      "description": "Public URL where agents reach the service.",
      "type": "array",
      "prefixItems": [{ "const": "url" }, { "type": "string", "format": "uri" }]
    },
    "about_tag": {
      "description": "Service summary, same as name tag.",
      "type": "array",
      "prefixItems": [{ "const": "about" }, { "type": "string" }]
    },
    "pmi_tag": {
      "description": "Payment method indicator. Always 'bitcoin-lightning-bolt11' for Aperture.",
      "type": "array",
      "prefixItems": [{ "const": "pmi" }, { "const": "bitcoin-lightning-bolt11" }]
    },
    "t_tag": {
      "description": "Topic tag for discoverability (l402, api, aperture).",
      "type": "array",
      "prefixItems": [{ "const": "t" }, { "type": "string" }]
    },
    "price_tag": {
      "description": "Positional price tag: [\"price\", capability, amount, currency].",
      "type": "array",
      "prefixItems": [
        { "const": "price" },
        { "type": "string", "description": "Capability or service name" },
        { "type": "string", "pattern": "^[0-9]+$", "description": "Price in smallest unit" },
        { "type": "string", "description": "Currency (e.g. 'sats')" }
      ],
      "minItems": 4,
      "maxItems": 4
    },
    "picture_tag": {
      "description": "Optional service icon URL.",
      "type": "array",
      "prefixItems": [{ "const": "picture" }, { "type": "string", "format": "uri" }]
    },
    "content_object": {
      "description": "The JSON object encoded in the content field.",
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "capabilities": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "object",
            "required": ["name", "description"],
            "properties": {
              "name": { "type": "string", "description": "Capability identifier" },
              "description": { "type": "string", "description": "Human-readable description" },
              "endpoint": { "type": "string", "description": "Cleaned base path for this capability (e.g. /v1/loop)" },
              "pricing": { "type": "string", "enum": ["dynamic"], "description": "Set to 'dynamic' when the price is determined at request time." },
              "auth": { "type": "string", "description": "Authentication level: 'none' (no auth), 'freebie N' (N free requests), or omitted (payment required)." },
              "timeout": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 0, "description": "L402 token validity in seconds. Omitted when no expiry." }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Example Output

Using a sample Aperture config with two services (loop-rpc and pool-rpc):

```json
{
  "kind": 31402,
  "tags": [
    ["d", "aperture-api.example.com"],
    ["name", "loop-rpc, pool-rpc"],
    ["url", "https://api.example.com"],
    ["about", "L402-gated API via Aperture — loop-rpc, pool-rpc"],
    ["pmi", "bitcoin-lightning-bolt11"],
    ["t", "l402"],
    ["t", "api"],
    ["t", "aperture"],
    ["price", "quote", "500", "sats"],
    ["price", "swap", "500", "sats"],
    ["price", "pool-rpc", "1000", "sats"]
  ],
  "content": "{\"capabilities\":[{\"name\":\"quote\",\"description\":\"quote via loop-rpc\",\"endpoint\":\"/v1/loop\"},{\"name\":\"swap\",\"description\":\"swap via loop-rpc\",\"endpoint\":\"/v1/loop\"},{\"name\":\"pool-rpc\",\"description\":\"Access pool-rpc\",\"endpoint\":\"/v1/pool\"}]}"
}
```

(Fields `id`, `pubkey`, `created_at`, and `sig` omitted — they vary per run.)

## Ecosystem

- [402-mcp](https://github.com/TheCryptoDonkey/402-mcp) — MCP client that discovers and pays for L402 APIs
- [402-announce](https://github.com/TheCryptoDonkey/402-announce) — TypeScript equivalent of this tool
- [toll-booth](https://github.com/TheCryptoDonkey/toll-booth) — Alternative L402 middleware
- [Aperture](https://github.com/lightninglabs/aperture) — The L402 reverse proxy this tool announces
